title: Ceraia bukidnonensis
intro: Ceraia bukidnonensis, also known as Bukidnon orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be useful in treating wounds and preventing infections. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the Bukidnon orchid is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Ceraia bukidnonensis is a species of moth in the family Sphingidae. It is a type of hawk moth that is endemic to the Philippines. The classification of Ceraia bukidnonensis includes its kingdom as Animalia, phylum Arthropoda, class Insecta, order Lepidoptera, and family Sphingidae. This species is part of the genus Ceraia, which is also known as the 'flame hawk moths'. Further classification information may be found in entomological databases and scientific literature.
uses: Ceraia bukidnonensis, a plant species native to the Philippines, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health. Traditional healers in the region where the plant grows have reported using it to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation. Some studies suggest that extracts from Ceraia bukidnonensis may have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Further studies are necessary to confirm these findings and explore the plant's full potential as a medicinal resource.

constituents: Ceraia bukidnonensis, a species of orchid, contains various bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.
